name: SAT / Constraint Solving

x.io: A set of Boolean (or richer) constraints → a satisfying assignment, or a proof of unsatisfiability.
preconditions: Willingness to encode the domain into CNF or a theory
correctness: Sound and complete: returns SAT with a model or UNSAT with a refutation
Modern solvers are decision procedures, not heuristics
x.stability: Deterministic given fixed solver settings; restarts/heuristics make timing vary.
x.parallelisable: Portfolio and clause-sharing parallel solvers exist; not trivially linear.

def: The engine behind solving NP-complete decision problems: modern CDCL SAT (and SMT) solvers decide satisfiability of huge constraint sets by learning from conflicts.
problem: Decide whether a large set of logical constraints can be satisfied — verification, planning, scheduling, configuration.
mechanism: Conflict-Driven Clause Learning: assign variables, propagate implications (unit propagation via watched literals), and on conflict, analyse it to learn a new clause and backjump non-chronologically. Restarts and activity-based heuristics (VSIDS) tame the search.
benefits: Decides industrial instances with millions of clauses
General: many problems reduce to SAT/SMT
limits: Opaque models; hard to predict runtime
failures: Naive encodings blow up clause count
whenNot: Optimisation (use ILP / MaxSAT) rather than pure decision
myths: "NP-complete means hopeless in practice" — CDCL solvers routinely crush real instances
